title: Report a Suspicious Message
summary: Flag a phishing or scam message through the proper channel.
learnerPromise: You will be able to recognize when a message should be reported and use the right tools to flag it.
whyItMatters: Reporting suspicious messages helps protect you and others from scams and phishing attempts. It also helps platforms improve their security and block harmful senders.
realLifeUses: You receive an email asking for your password that looks fake
A text message claims to be from your bank but seems suspicious
A social media message contains a strange link from someone you know
You get a phone call or voicemail asking for personal information
materialsNeeded: A phone or computer with the suspicious message visible
Access to the app, email service, or website where you received it
result: A suspicious message reported through the proper channel and deleted from your inbox.
youWillLearn: How to identify which messages are appropriate to report
How to use the built-in report button in email, text, and social media apps
How to forward phishing emails to your organization's IT or your email provider's abuse address
How to delete the message safely after reporting it
miniChallenge: Find or imagine a suspicious message and practice reporting it using the report feature in your app or email service.
steps: Open the suspicious message but do not click any links or download any attachments
Look for a report button, flag icon, or 'Report spam/junk' option in your app or email service
Tap or click the report option and follow any prompts — some services ask why you are reporting
If the message is a phishing email at work or school, forward it to your IT department as well
Delete the message from your inbox and, if possible, block the sender
completionCriteria: You identified a suspicious message and used a report or flag feature
You deleted the message and know how to find the report feature again in the future
commonProblems: Cannot find the report button — look in the message menu or near the delete button
Not sure if the message is really a scam — when in doubt, report it; better safe than sorry
Reported a message by accident — most reports do not cause problems for the sender unless a pattern exists
tips: Never click links or reply to a suspicious message — reporting is enough
If a message claims to be from a company you use, contact that company directly through their official website
Reporting spam and scams helps the platform protect other users too
Take a screenshot before deleting if you need to share it with someone for advice
